mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate reduces discount rates and supports equity multiples, so month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS has fallen versus the prior month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated policy rate above neutral constrains liquidity and compresses valuation multiples, so FEDFUNDS above 4.5% predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and tighter credit conditions, which pressure earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in payroll growth and retail sales signals stronger final demand and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-plus-RSAFS signal does not outperform either proxy alone in 5-day directional accuracy, or if positive returns are not more frequent when both series are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production reflects improving output and operating leverage, which supports cyclical earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO are not associated with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts slump below a low-activity threshold indicates weakening construction demand and spillovers to cyclicals, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-HOUST months do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the conditional average return is not meaningfully below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth weakens while employment remains soft, the combination identifies a late-cycle demand slowdown that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent weakness in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not improve prediction versus either series alone, or if negative 60-day returns are not the modal outcome in low-growth, low-payroll states.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation deceleration combined with a still-restrictive policy rate eases real-rate pressure and supports equity valuation expansion, so a falling CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS above 5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-restrictive-policy episodes fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either proxy alone.
